CVE-2016-2781.title: chroot in GNU coreutils, when used with --userspec, allows local  ...
CVE-2016-2781.description: chroot in GNU coreutils, when used with --userspec, allows local users to escape to the parent session via a crafted TIOCSTI ioctl call, which pushes characters to the terminal's input buffer.
CVE-2016-2781.notes:
CVE-2016-2781 affects GNU coreutils chroot when used with --userspec, allowing local users to escape to the parent session via a crafted TIOCSTI ioctl. Restricting ioctl on the kernel side was considered but rejected by Linux upstream. Fixing this issue via setsid() would introduce regressions. Since Linux 6.4.4-1 (23 Jul 2023), TIOCSTI was disabled in the kernel, and the first coreutils release after that, 9.4-1, is marked as the fixed version. 
Remediation suggestions:
• Remove chroot if unused
